response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer whose career has been built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web technologies. He has negligible C++ experience — roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project — with no evidence of sustained, production-grade Windows C++ development. He has no demonstrable background in capital markets, fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or investment banking systems (RIDE Capital is a fintech/tax-wrapper investment product, not a capital markets trading environment). There is no mention of SQL Server, Oracle, Summit platform, or financial-domain tooling. The mandatory requirements of this role are fundamentally unmet.
strengths: Demonstrated communication and knowledge-sharing ability through multiple public workshops and conference talks
Broad exposure to CI/CD pipelines, DevOps tooling (GitLab CI, Docker, Artifactory), and modern engineering practices
Some Java experience and brief C/C++ work at National Instruments, showing familiarity with compiled, lower-level languages
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months professionally) and not on Windows — the role requires 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development
No capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge; primary financial exposure is a fintech startup unrelated to trading systems
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency; SQL appears only as a listed skill without depth or enterprise-scale context
